featured.description: White-label engine for digital asset marketplaces. A unique, scalable architecture that accelerates Time-to-Market, proven in production by more than seven clients across radically different markets.

items.description: Blockchain-anchored document certification and traceability, designed for real-world adoption by non-technical teams.
Secure document management for notaries, built for sensitive exchanges without burdening users' daily workflow.
Wealth management platform for Financial Advisors and Back-Office teams, tailored for two profiles with completely different business logic.

about.label: About

skills.title: Product & UX
skills.description: End-to-end interfaces that anticipate complex cases and the errors that make all the difference in production.

skills.title: Design Systems
skills.description: A structured, scalable and documented system that allows scaling and maintaining total consistency with every new feature.

skills.title: Design to Code
skills.description: A smooth transition to development, ensuring that design decisions hold all the way to production.

body: I design web and product interfaces for high-stakes environments such as fintech, wealth management, and security.

My dual design and front-end culture allows me to integrate technical constraints from the design phase, delivering files that are directly ready to implement, ensuring a seamless handoff to production.

process.label: Process
process.heading: How I work.
process.description: Four key steps to align product vision, design and development.

steps.title: Framing
steps.description: Laying the strategic foundations of the product to align business objectives and user expectations from the start.
steps.label: //Step 01

steps.title: Architecture
steps.description: Structuring product logic upstream of the screens to directly align design components and code.
steps.label: //Step 02

steps.title: Alignment
steps.description: Designing and iterating in fast cycles, testing every decision against product priorities and code realities.
steps.label: //Step 03

steps.title: Handoff
steps.description: Delivering specifications and mockups ready for production, with no room for interpretation.
steps.label: //Step 04

contact.label: Contact
contact.heading: Let's design your project.
contact.description: Describe your project in a few words, I reply fast.

form.briefPlaceholder: What is the main goal of your product and your current constraints (timeline, technical, design system…)?

errors.briefRequired: Describe your project in a few words.

intro.description: A B2B financial distribution SaaS designed for two profiles with diverging needs. Its single interface adapts the flows of Financial Advisors and the Back-Office while unifying subscription tracking.

context.label: Context
context.heading: One dataset shared between two profiles.
context.p1: Wenimmo is a B2B financial distribution SaaS. Financial Advisors initiate subscriptions and manage the client relationship, while the Back-Office oversees compliance and file validation.
context.p2: The challenge: making these diverging flows coexist within a single tool without burdening the experience.

problem.label: Problem
problem.heading: Balancing speed and rigor.
problem.p1: The Financial Advisor works on the go, between client meetings. Their priority is immediacy: managing files and initiating a subscription in a few clicks. Conversely, the Back-Office handles volume and requires density: advanced filters, granular pipeline, and complete administrative tracking.
problem.p2: The whole challenge was to design a single system capable of switching its UI priorities based on the logged-in profile, without building two separate products.

solution.label: Solution
solution.heading: One platform for two workflows.
solution.p1: The platform relies on a single database to guarantee total synchronization. The interface dynamically adjusts to the profile by modifying its visual density, priorities, and actions.
solution.p2: On one side, a linear flow guides the Financial Advisor through to signature. On the other, a dense pipeline is optimized for the Back-Office's bulk processing.

features.title: Tailored design
features.description: The application loads a dedicated environment based on the account type, adapted to each team's workflows without visual overload.

features.title: Synchronized flows
features.description: Each action is instantly reflected across account types, providing a common reference for both teams.

features.title: Invisible compliance
features.description: KYC and electronic signature are embedded into the funnel and run in the background to maintain flow continuity.

cgp.label: Financial Advisor profile
cgp.heading:
Between client meetings,
on the go.
cgp.description: Designed for mobile use, the interface allows the Financial Advisor to instantly check a file status, initiate a subscription, or validate a document. The system preserves context between sessions: resuming an interrupted flow requires no reconstruction effort.

features.title: Flow branching
features.description: The investor status (individual/company) is set at entry and routes the flow, eliminating data entry errors.

features.title: Progress visibility
features.description: The stepper makes progress predictable through a dense funnel, cuts cognitive load, and makes it easy to resume a file.

features.title: Business-oriented statuses
features.description: Statuses replace technical naming with field language, serving team needs rather than server state.

features.title: Irreversible actions
features.description: The RTO agreement and final send commit the Advisor. The interface isolates these actions for their regulatory weight.

middleOffice.label: Back-Office profile
middleOffice.heading: Process in bulk, nothing lost.
middleOffice.description: The Back-Office orchestrates dozens of complex subscriptions with constantly changing statuses every day. The interface adapts in real time to streamline bulk flows, automate repetitive tasks, and eliminate operational error risks.

features.title: Centralized view
features.description: The list view centralizes all files to manage every flow, regardless of which Financial Advisor originated them.

features.title: Contextual actions
features.description: Whether following up, flagging, or validating, actions adapt to the file status to eliminate any impossible operation.

features.title: Volume control
features.description: Advanced filters and multi-selection speed up bulk processing without sacrificing the traceability of each file.

features.title: Confirmed closure
features.description: Once validated by the operator, this final status locks the visual and permanently freezes the subscription history.

impact.label: Impact
impact.heading: Productivity multiplied.
impact.description: Unifying the flow removes the barrier between front and back-office. A single interface now orchestrates two profiles with opposing objectives, guaranteeing total operational fluidity.

features.title: For Financial Advisors
features.description: Flow interruptions no longer break the work stream. Visual tracking allows resuming any in-progress file, permanently eliminating data entry friction.

features.title: For the Back-Office
features.description: Security and fluidity guide bulk processing. Contextual actions and integrated traceability eliminate data entry errors and drastically streamline validations.

intro.description: A B2B2C notarial certification SaaS designed for sensitive document exchanges. Its interface aligns the notary's rigor requirements with the clients' need for clarity.

context.label: Context
context.heading: A simple and secure notarial management.
context.p1: The platform centralizes and automates the collection of these sensitive documents. From their space, the notary controls each received document before securing the final file through blockchain anchoring.
context.p2: On the other side, the end client has a guided flow to submit their supporting documents and track their file progress independently.

problem.label: Problem
problem.heading: Client clarity, notary control.
problem.p1: Notarial document collection traditionally suffers from manual follow-ups, scattered files, and a lack of formal tracking. Faced with this complexity, designing a single portal would have required too many compromises.
problem.p2: The notary demands high data density and full control, while the client requires absolute clarity. Merging these asymmetric expectations would inevitably have degraded one party's experience or the other's.

solution.label: Solution
solution.heading: Three spaces for a single file.
solution.p1: Three distinct spaces rely on a single data source to allow the notary to manage, the client to submit, and the administration to supervise access rights.
solution.p2: This separation guarantees strict functional isolation. By eliminating any global compromise, each environment is calibrated to its user's priorities without ever degrading the experience of the other parties.

features.title: Separate spaces
features.description: The notary has a dense view for their expertise, while the client benefits from a clean, distraction-free flow.

features.title: Progressive statuses
features.description: A visual pipeline replaces vague states with clear steps, dramatically reducing follow-up requests.

features.title: Integrated blockchain
features.description: Blockchain anchoring integrates into the notary's workflow and becomes a transparent action with instant visual feedback.

notaire.label: Notary space
notaire.heading: Centralizing file management.
notaire.p1: The notary's space centralizes the entire lifecycle of the file, from the document request to the final certification. It is the platform's most information-dense environment, designed to validate each document and trigger blockchain anchoring in one click.
notaire.p2: Management relies on a dynamic status system (empty, in progress, completed, action required, validated, anchored) that reflects the exact progress of the file in real time.

client.label: Client space
client.heading: Stripping the interface to guide submission.
client.p1: The client environment eliminates all cognitive load to focus exclusively on the expected action: knowing which document to submit and where, without being burdened by the complexity of the management system.
client.p2: After a secure login via FranceConnect, a guided flow takes over. The interface clearly separates validated documents from those still awaited by the office, providing immediate visibility into remaining tasks.

admin.label: Admin space
admin.heading: Secure governance through voting.
admin.p1: The administration space centralizes control of notarial offices through three distinct permission levels, designed to finely segment responsibilities.
admin.p2: To protect sensitive data, the Super-Admin role integrates a mandatory voting system. This design decision ensures that no critical modification of ecosystem rights can be validated unilaterally by a single user.

impact.label: Impact
impact.heading: Zero training, zero follow-up.
impact.description: The interface eliminates the learning curve for profiles with asymmetric usage. By compartmentalizing spaces, each stakeholder has an environment calibrated to their operational objectives, with no unnecessary visual noise.

features.title: For notaries
features.description: No more manual follow-ups lost in email threads. The interface centralizes real-time file tracking, provides total visibility, and enables follow-up or certification in one click.

features.title: For clients
features.description: Complete autonomy from the start. FranceConnect removes authentication friction, the flow shows required documents, and the client is notified at every step.

intro.description: A B2B blockchain certification SaaS designed for non-technical teams. Its dual-reading architecture gives direct access to cryptographic proofs without overloading the main interface.

context.label: Context
context.heading: Making blockchain proof accessible.
context.p1: The platform generates cryptographic fingerprints of files to prove their existence and integrity in an immutable way.
context.p2: The entire design challenge was to make this technical anchoring transparent for business teams (legal, HR, compliance) who daily deposit and verify these sensitive documents.

problem.label: Problem
problem.heading: Blockchain, safe but unreadable.
problem.p1: Raw cryptographic data remains indecipherable for business teams, even though it is essential for technical profiles.
problem.p2: The challenge was to manage this asymmetry: how to confirm a file's integrity to a non-technical user without hiding or altering the raw proof required by developers?

solution.label: Solution
solution.heading: Technical language into business language.
solution.p1: The solution structures this dual reading around four key flows: oversight via the dashboard, traceability via the history, action via deposit, and doubt removal via verification.
solution.p2: This approach prioritizes information to display the essentials by default and makes technical data accessible on demand. Interface statuses are named to answer a concrete user question, not to describe a system state.

decisions.label: Decisions
decisions.heading: Guiding the business eye.
decisions.description: Every display choice was driven by a strict information hierarchy. The experience rests on three major trade-offs, designed to guide the business profile's attention without ever overloading the interface.

features.title: Hiding complexity
features.description: Cryptographic data is removed from the main view. The interface focuses on one question: did the deposit succeed?

features.title: Group by batch and space
features.description: Multiple files deposited together form a single entry in the history. Spaces then segment these deposits by business context.

features.title: Two reading levels
features.description: Operational teams follow the main flow. Technical profiles access raw data via a drawer, without cluttering the general view.

dashboard.label: Dashboard
dashboard.heading: Simplifying the reading of data.
dashboard.description: The dashboard centralizes volume indicators and flow status to immediately isolate certification anomalies. Designed for operational oversight rather than simple consultation, this space surfaces urgent actions and anchoring failures. The interface protects the user from cognitive overload, while keeping the complete history available at the second reading level.

deposits.label: Deposits
deposits.heading: Clarifying deposit tracking
deposits.description: To absorb large volumes of documents, the history segments activity by thematic contexts. The main view favors a quick status read of the files, while a side panel concentrates technical tracking and anchoring proofs, accessible on demand.

items.title: Securing certification submission
items.description: The deposit funnel processes multiple files simultaneously. The interface shows a real-time waiting time estimate and generates a pre-filled support ticket in case of a blockage.

items.title: Making verification instant
items.description: This flow simplifies access to blockchain data. The application instantly analyzes the document to deliver a clear visual status, allowing authenticity verification at a glance without dealing with technical complexity.

impact.label: Impact
impact.heading: Optimizing operational efficiency.
impact.description: By eliminating friction related to reading technical data, the interface removes the historical time loss of certification. Information adapts to the user profile to deliver the exact reading level needed, ensuring autonomy for both business teams and developers.

features.title: Business teams
features.description: No more need to ask an expert to validate a deposit. Statuses are readable without blockchain knowledge, progress is displayed in real time, and support remains accessible directly in the interface.

features.title: Technical teams
features.description: No more searching for a hash: everything is centralized in the deposit detail. The CSV export covers audit needs, reinforcing user autonomy to drastically reduce tickets.

intro.description: A modular white-label Web3 marketplace. Designed to adapt to each client, the interface reconfigures instantly without touching the code.

context.label: Context
context.heading: Aligning design and code.
context.p1: Deploying a single architecture under radically different identities constantly exposes the platform to technical debt. Without a strict semantic structure, each client customization undermines the overall product maintenance.
context.p2: To absorb opposing worlds, from gaming to finance, the system relies on dynamic theming. Design and code share the same variables, eliminating adjustment back-and-forth and guaranteeing interface integrity at every deployment.

problem.label: Problem
problem.heading: The technical cost of customization.
problem.p1: What varies from one client to another is limited to surface values: colors, typography, border radius, or spacing. Behaviors, hierarchy, and accessibility remain strictly unchanged.
problem.p2: Separating these two layers makes it possible to roll out dozens of identities without impacting the component structure. More than a visual optimization, it is the fundamental technical choice that makes white-label viable at scale.

solution.label: Solution
solution.heading: One system for all brands.
solution.p1: To industrialize the product from a single base, the solution relies on a three-layer architecture: fixed primitive values, client-configurable semantic tokens, and agnostic components.
solution.p2: The technical integration is done once so the UI adapts dynamically. What used to require months of research and development now integrates with a new client in one week.

decision.label: Decision
decision.heading: Industrializing multi-brand.
decision.description: Bloom started without a semantic layer out of pragmatism. As the product grew, this technical debt required three weeks of rework to restructure the system. For a scalable product, this intermediate layer is an essential foundation from the start, not a late optimization.

features.title: Limits of the raw model
features.description: Binding components to raw values speeds up the start, but accumulates technical debt with every client variation.

features.title: The semantic ecosystem
features.description: An intermediate layer translates primitives according to their context. Changing a client's identity comes down to modifying a few variables.

architecture.label: Architecture
architecture.heading: Modular components.
architecture.description: Some clients impose unforeseen constraints. A component that is too rigid forces a fork; too generic, it becomes useless. The answer relies on fixed components, broken into variants capable of absorbing these specificities.

layers.title: Raw values
layers.description: Each raw value is defined without semantic intent: the technical foundation is set, but remains disconnected from the product.
layers.label: //layer 1

layers.title: Semantic roles
layers.description: Each primitive receives a functional role. The identity is applied at this level, without impacting the component architecture.
layers.label: //layer 2

layers.title: Final components
layers.description: Each component only consumes these semantic tokens. Consistency remains total, regardless of the client's identity.
layers.label: //layer 3

alignment.label: Tech alignment
alignment.heading: Designing for production.
alignment.p1: Component states follow CSS pseudo-classes: the structure of a token like btn-primary-bg-default corresponds exactly to the code.
alignment.p2: The technical spec being integrated into Figma, the developer implements the mockup without any interpretation. No component is rewritten from scratch: the architecture is self-sufficient.

identities.label: Identities
identities.heading: One structure, multiple identities
identities.p1: The component and token architecture is identical for everyone. To deploy a new client, it is enough to inject their primitives (colors, typography, border radius) via a configuration file.
identities.p2: The identity propagates instantly to the entire platform. On the code side, the developer applies the theme in one line, without touching the components.

method.label: Method
method.heading:
Built alone.
Built to be handed off.
method.p1: Working alone requires absolute rigor in documentation: naming, justifying, and annotating every decision to streamline production by the developer who will implement the interface.
method.p2: The system must be understandable without opening Figma. Components are structured by usage state and tokens carry a semantic name rather than a raw value.

features.title: A self-sufficient deliverable
features.description: The deliverable does not need to be explained in a meeting. Annotations integrated during the design capture the real intent, eliminating after-the-fact corrections.

features.title: Naming modeled on code
features.description: Design system and code share the same naming conventions. The developer immediately finds their bearings, turning the handoff into a simple synchronization.

items.title: Configuring an asset issuance
items.description: From a single piece to a full collection, the creator configures their digital assets in a few clicks. They define volume, price, and rarity through a fluid journey that hides all the technical complexity of the blockchain.

items.title: Streamlining the purchase to wallet
items.description: A three-step purchase funnel: product page, payment, and secure transfer. Each interaction is designed to reassure and eliminate friction related to digital transactions.

playground.label: Playground
playground.heading: The design system in action
playground.lead: Select a brand to see the entire system reconfigure.

meta.home: Web and product interface design (fintech, SaaS, marketing sites). Design & front-end rigour for Figma files ready to implement, with no loss in production.
meta.bloom: Lead Product Designer on a white-label Web3 marketplace. Modular architecture, multi-client design system, production delivery.
meta.keepro: Product Designer on a B2B blockchain certification SaaS. Cryptographic proof made readable for non-technical teams.
meta.lecoffre: Product Designer on a B2B2C notarial platform. Three distinct spaces, zero manual follow-up, integrated blockchain anchoring.
